title: Topology stabilized fluctuations in a magnetic nodal semimetal
type1_summary: The Excel workbook is figure-linked, tabular, and directly replot-ready, so it is Type 1 cleaned/source data.
type2_summary: No clearly identifiable raw/unprocessed data files are present in the inspection report.
classification_notes: The paper discusses raw measurements, but the provided file set does not include obvious raw instrument outputs. The workbook appears to be curated source data deposited for figures, while the PDFs are supplementary documentation rather than data tables.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper states that supplementary information and a peer review file are available; this PDF is consistent with a supplementary document rather than a data table. No explicit evidence that it contains figure-ready source data.
file_classifications.file_evidence: PDF format, 4.1 MB, from nature_source_data. No sheet/column structure is available, so it is not directly inspectable as tabular replot-ready data from the file report.
file_classifications.reasoning: Given only the inspection report, this is best treated as supplementary documentation rather than a dataset file. There is insufficient evidence to classify it as raw or cleaned data.
file_classifications.ambiguity: High: the PDF could contain supplementary figures, methods, or source-data explanations, but the report does not expose its contents.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: PDF document; no tabular structure reported

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper mentions supplementary information and a peer review file. This PDF is consistent with an accompanying document, not clearly a data file.
file_classifications.file_evidence: PDF format, 4.6 MB, from nature_source_data. No tabular metadata or sheet structure is reported.
file_classifications.reasoning: Without content-level evidence, the conservative classification is documentation rather than data. It may contain supplementary text/figures, but that is not enough to call it Type 1 or Type 2.
file_classifications.ambiguity: High: content not visible in the inspection report.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: PDF document; no tabular structure reported

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper explicitly states that the data used in the study are available in Figshare. The workbook contains figure-linked sheets for transport, heat capacity, REXS, dilatometry, and neutron-related data, matching the paper's source-data/figure-data style.
file_classifications.file_evidence: Excel workbook with 18 sheets and explicit figure/supplement references in sheet names, e.g. '(1,2,3,4,5,S2,S7) ρxx, xy - T', '(1) C - T', '(1, 5, S15) REXS sigma-pi at K', '(2, 3, S4, S5, S7) ρxx, xy - B', '(2, 3, 5, S6, S7) κxx, xy - B', '(2, 3) ΔL - B'. Columns are organized as measured variables with units and are directly plottable with minimal processing.
file_classifications.reasoning: Although the underlying measurements are raw experimental observations in the paper, the workbook structure is figure-oriented and cleaned for plotting. The sheet names map to specific figures/supplementary figures, and the columns are already in analysis-ready form. By the dataset definition, this is Type 1 source/cleaned data rather than raw instrument output.
file_classifications.ambiguity: Low: some columns are measured quantities, but the organization and naming clearly indicate curated source data.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: Sheets include Temperature (K), Magnetic Field (Oe), Resistivity (Ohm-m), Sample Temp. (K), Samp HC (mJ/mole-K), H/K/L, Seconds, amptek_r2, Conductivity (W/K-m), Seebeck Coef. (µV/K), etc.

paper_analysis.summary: CeAlGe, a non-centrosymmetric magnetic Weyl semimetal candidate, is shown to host a narrow temperature window of locally correlated magnetic fluctuations well above its thermodynamic magnetic transition at TN = 4.5 K. Using resonant elastic X-ray scattering, electrical/thermal transport, dilatometry, and neutron scattering, the authors argue that these fluctuations are stabilized by coupling between Weyl fermions and local moments, with the observed ordering wavevector matching Weyl-node nesting conditions.
measurement_types: ab initio calculations / electronic structure calculations

figures.description: Crystal structure schematic of CeAlGe in space group I41md, showing Ce-Ce bonds and broken inversion symmetry.
Longitudinal resistivity versus temperature at different magnetic fields applied along c.
Heat-capacity difference between CeAlGe and LaAlGe at 0 T and 9 T, with integrated entropy curves.
Calculated Weyl-node locations in the Brillouin zone and the relation of the magnetic wavevector to Weyl-node nesting.
Schematic of nesting between Weyl cones and coupling to a localized magnetic ion.
REXS intensity map in the sigma-pi channel showing an incommensurate magnetic peak at negative K values over temperature.
REXS intensity map in the sigma-pi channel showing the corresponding incommensurate peak at positive K values, with temperature-dependent integrated peak intensity inset.
Phase-diagram estimate from the second derivative of longitudinal resistivity with respect to magnetic field.
Phase-diagram estimate from the second derivative of thermal conductivity with respect to magnetic field.
Phase-diagram estimate from dilatometry / thermal expansion measurements, showing a distinct regime above TN.
Schematic assignment of magnetic phases and fluctuation regime as a function of temperature and field.
Field-dependent electrical and thermal conductivities at T = 2 K, showing transitions between magnetic regimes.
Magnetoresistance versus temperature/field showing crossover from positive to negative MR near 12.8 K.
Field suppression of thermal conductivity at 12.8 K, including comparison of maximal changes in thermal conductivity and resistivity.
Low-temperature dilation measurements.
Magnetoresistance versus applied magnetic field at T = 12.8 K, showing near-zero MR.
Longitudinal resistivity versus temperature/field with a Kondo-like fit highlighting a resistivity minimum near 20 K.
Schematic of scattering between two Weyl nodes of the same chirality, distinguishing intraband and interband processes.
Map of Weyl-node separations and measured magnetic wavevector in momentum space.
figures.likely_source: theoretical/compiled from prior calculations plus experimental marker

figures.description: Decomposition of scattering vectors relative to the magnetic propagation-vector basis.
Calculated second-order free-energy contribution as a function of scattering-vector orientation in the magnetic basis.
Calculated free energy versus temperature for different finite-domain cutoffs, showing stabilization over a wider temperature range for smaller domains.
Summary plot comparing inverse MR deviation, maximal field-induced thermal-conductivity deviation, and REXS intensity versus temperature in the fluctuation-stabilization regime.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Yes. The paper reports raw measurements from single-crystal and powder experiments: longitudinal resistivity and thermal conductivity in a PPMS Dynacool, dilatometry in a capacitive dilatometer, resonant elastic X-ray scattering at NSLS-II beamline 4-ID near the Ce L2 edge, elastic and inelastic neutron scattering on SEQUOIA, HB-1/PTAX, and HB-3/TAX, powder X-ray diffraction for lattice constants, and Raman measurements. The text also mentions co-aligned single crystals and specific incident energies/fields for neutron experiments.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states: 'The data used in this study are available in the Figshare database under accession code https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.23576637.' It also notes that supplementary information contains additional details, and that a peer review file is available.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are implied to be the direct instrument outputs from transport, dilatometry, REXS, neutron scattering, powder XRD, and Raman measurements. The paper does not specify file formats, directory structure, or whether raw detector/event files are included in Figshare.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data clearly include derived quantities such as second derivatives of resistivity and thermal conductivity, symmetrized dilatometry, integrated REXS peak intensities normalized to a structural Bragg peak, entropy obtained by cumulative integration of heat capacity, magnetoresistance, and fitted resistivity curves using a Kondo phenomenological model.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-ready data are definitely present in the sense that the paper reports plotted traces, maps, and inset summaries for the main figures. However, the text does not explicitly state whether separate source-data files per figure are included in the repository.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is multimodal and mixes direct measurements with derived/processed quantities and theory curves. Several figures rely on post-processing: numerical derivatives for phase maps, Lorentzian peak fitting with background subtraction for REXS, symmetrization of dilatometry, and model fitting of resistivity. The paper explicitly distinguishes experimental observations from theoretical effective-Hamiltonian calculations, but does not describe any software package or script distribution. The Figshare link is the only explicit data repository statement in the text.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The Methods section lists multiple direct measurements performed on instruments (PPMS Dynacool, NSLS-II beamline 4-ID, SEQUOIA, HB-1/HB-3, dilatometer), indicating raw experimental data were collected.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: Figure captions and Results describe derived quantities such as second derivatives, integrated peak intensities, entropy from heat-capacity integration, symmetrized dilatometry, and fitted resistivity curves, which are typical source/processed data products.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “The data used in this study are available in the Figshare database under accession code https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.23576637.”; “Error bars represent one standard deviation and are obtained through propagation of errors from counting statistics and Lorentzian peak fitting.”; “The entropy S ... is extracted through cumulative integration of the heat capacity.”; “The resulting dilatometry was symmetrized.”
